message:
So last weekend I was making pasta and got distracted (shocking, I know), and the oil started smoking pretty aggressively. Grabbed the pan, opened a window, and—totally normally—my smoke alarm went off. My first thought wasn't panic, it was actually relief. That little chirp meant it was *working*.

Turned into this funny moment where my kids came running like we'd just discovered buried treasure, and I realized I hadn't actually talked them through what to do in a real fire. So I did something kinda nice instead of scary: we made it a little game. I showed them where our exits are, we drew a simple map together, and I let them help me check the batteries in our alarms. They felt like safety detectives.

It reminded me that these conversations don't have to feel heavy or doom-y. Just... normal family time that actually matters.

Do you have a smoke alarm moment that caught you off guard in a good way?
